description: The image displays a three-dimensional ball-and-stick model of a cubic crystal structure representing the Fe–Mn–Al alloy system investigated via first-principles calculations. The supercell visualization features three distinct atomic species represented by colored spheres—pink/purple, gray, and green—corresponding to Fe, Mn, and Al atoms respectively. This structural model illustrates the B2-ordered cubic phase (space group Pm-3m) with atoms occupying specific lattice sites including corner and body-centered positions. The figure visualizes the crystallographic framework used to calculate formation enthalpy, cohesive energy, electronic density of states, and elastic constants across the compositional series from FeAl to MnAl. It demonstrates the atomic coordination environment and substitution patterns of Mn for Fe in the intermetallic lattice, providing the structural basis for understanding the mechanical anisotropy, bonding characteristics (covalent and metallic), and thermodynamic stability reported in the density functional theory study.
